task_completion.description: Open the base design dual_parachute_deployment.ork in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket application is open with the file 'dual_parachute_deployment' visible in the title bar or component tree.

task_completion.description: Add or configure a transition component with a forward diameter of 101.6mm and aft diameter of 57.4mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Transition configuration window is visible showing a forward diameter of 101.6 mm (or 4 in), aft diameter of 57.4 mm, and length of ~150 mm, or the component tree shows a transition matching this description.

task_completion.description: Add or configure a booster body tube with 57.4mm OD and ~600mm length, equipped with a 54mm motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Body Tube configuration window showing OD 57.4 mm and length ~600 mm, and a motor configuration tab or component tree showing a 54mm motor loaded.

task_completion.description: Design fins to achieve a stability margin of at least 1.0 calibers and run a flight simulation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket main window shows a stability margin of >= 1.0 cal in the upper right corner, and the 'Flight simulations' tab shows a completed simulation run.

task_completion.description: Save the modified design as reconstructed_rocket.ork.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title changes to 'reconstructed_rocket.ork' or a save dialog is visible saving to this filename in /home/ga/Documents/rockets/.

task_completion.description: Document the original and new booster diameters, motor selected, and stability margin in reconstruction_memo.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of 'reconstruction_memo.txt' with the required engineering details (original/new diameters, motor, and stability margin).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenRocket GUI being actively used (configuration dialogs, component tree edits), rather than the agent manually editing the .ork XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The flight simulation was genuinely executed in OpenRocket.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab shows actual simulation results (e.g., apogee, velocity) rather than being empty or unrun.

integrity.description: The memo contents reflect the actual values from the OpenRocket session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The stability margin and motor details in the text file match the values seen in the OpenRocket GUI screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target transition must have a forward radius of 0.0508m (101.6mm diameter) and an aft radius of 0.0287m (57.4mm diameter). The booster length must be ~0.6m (600mm). The stability margin must be >= 1.0 calibers. The modified design must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/reconstructed_rocket.ork and the memo to /home/ga/Documents/exports/reconstruction_memo.txt. Note: The base model actually has a max diameter of ~56.6mm, so the instruction to build a transition with a forward diameter of 101.6mm forces a physically nonsensical step-up transition. Do not penalize the agent for this visual anomaly as long as the dimensions match the instructions.
